pose_sweep.note: UI-driven pose sweep may under-report eye close; use automation smoke for parameter support before final judgment.

ParamAngleX.v21_action: include in v21 smoke, then validate through real Cubism deformer hierarchy later

ParamEyeBallX.reason: moves coherent generated iris+pupil+highlight detail layers while fixed whites stay still
ParamEyeBallX.v21_action: keep and tune movement strength only if 주인님 sees drift

ParamEyeBallY.v21_action: keep and tune movement strength only if 주인님 sees drift

ParamEyeLOpen.reason: keypose/opacity eye close is clamped at min 0.27; 0.0 is intentionally not exposed
ParamEyeLOpen.v21_action: keep; use automation/canvas proof because UI pose sweep can under-report eye close

ParamEyeROpen.reason: keypose/opacity eye close is clamped at min 0.27; 0.0 is intentionally not exposed
ParamEyeROpen.v21_action: keep; use automation/canvas proof because UI pose sweep can under-report eye close

ParamHairFront.reason: front_hair_warp has no child parts; slider cannot visibly move hair
ParamHairFront.v21_action: remove from active v21 smoke or create/separate real front hair parts first

ParamMouthOpenY.reason: mouth opens through diagnostic keypose opacity/mouth_warp, not final phoneme-ready Cubism mouth rig
ParamMouthOpenY.v21_action: keep for v21 smoke; production mouth still needs final visual acceptance

v21_scope.first_v21_task: build a supported-controls-only Mini Cubism rig smoke/review packet from v20 and hide or clearly mark unsupported HairFront

real_cubism_blockers: ParamHairFront is contract-only because no independent front hair parts are present
v20 has 36 diagnostic parts, below the confirmed v2_standard 64-part production target
Mini Cubism diagnostic PASS does not prove real Cubism ArtMesh/Deformer/Keyform authoring
mouth is still diagnostic keypose/crossfade and needs human visual acceptance before production mouth rigging
decision: Proceed to v21 Mini Cubism supported-control rig smoke, not final Cubism authoring, unless 주인님 first accepts v20 human visual QA.
